unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Reader macros, dotted-pair source notation, character literals, decimal literal tokens, and every other experimental reader extension
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: The frozen lexer/parser recognizes only the declared GC-AGENT-v0.3 grammar.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Use the lexical-grammar surface in the frozen profile and represent decimals through dec/parse.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Mutable variables, objects, collections, or ambient process state in kernel evaluation
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: The kernel is pure and values are immutable.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Return a new persistent value and thread it explicitly through pure functions.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Forged or unsealed UNHANDLED, EFFECT, or ERROR protocol values
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: Protocol recognition requires unforgeable Prelude seal tokens.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Construct protocol outcomes only through the sealed Prelude protocol helpers.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Direct or implicit filesystem, clock, entropy, network, process, GPU, model, or LLM host operations inside source or kernel evaluation
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: Host-only operations are outside the pure kernel and require a runner-mediated effect.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Emit a declared effect operation, authorize it minimally in caps.toml, and require run/replay equivalence.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Any capability absent from the negotiated profile or automatically authorized merely because an operation appears in a host index
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: Indices describe availability; selected profiles bound availability; caps.toml alone grants scoped authority.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Negotiate a profile that contains the operation and add the minimum explicit caps.toml grant as a separately reviewable policy change.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Unbounded package evaluation when limits.allow_unlimited is absent or false
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: The default package policy rejects disabling the step limit.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Declare finite package and effect budgets; use allow_unlimited only in an explicitly reviewed trusted profile.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: A stable or compatibility-authoritative bytecode format
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: The v1 bytecode compatibility reservation is intentionally unbound.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Use canonical CoreForm and validated current execution tiers until a versioned bytecode is frozen.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: General source-to-Wasm coverage or undeclared stage2 fallback
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: Stage2 translation supports only a validated subset and must fail closed outside it.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Negotiate the exact stage2 coverage profile or execute through a complete verified lower tier.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: A production JIT or native optimizing backend
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: No validated JIT tier exists in the current profile.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Use treewalk, compiled AST, or another explicitly validated lower execution tier.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Native, browser, edge, WASI, iOS, Android, OCI, component, or other build/deploy targets not explicitly marked available by the negotiated target profile
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: GC-AGENT-v0.3 language membership does not imply that a target runtime, SDK, or production pipeline is available.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Select a target profile with verified availability and declared limitations, or remain on the portable local execution profile.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Ambient wall clock, unrecorded entropy/randomness, unordered scheduling, race-selected outcomes, or replay facts omitted from logs
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: Nondeterministic facilities are not kernel primitives; all observable scheduling and host facts belong in deterministic logs.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: Use deterministic scheduling or explicit logged effects whose complete facts are checked during replay.

unsupportedBehavior.behavior: Unqualified v1 compatibility or production-readiness claims
unsupportedBehavior.rationale: All v1 identifiers are reserved-not-stable until the R9 release freeze.
unsupportedBehavior.safeAlternative: State the exact pre-v1 profile, maturity, target limitations, and evidence level.
